heading: Rights-of-way for dams, reservoirs, or water plants for municipal, mining, and milling purposes
label: 524 Rights-of-way for dams, reservoirs, or water plants for municipal, mining, and milling purposes
text: Rights-of-way for the construction and maintenance of dams, reservoirs, water plants, ditches, flumes, pipes, tunnels, and canals, within and across the national forests of the United States, are granted to citizens and corporations of the United States for municipal or mining purposes, and for the purposes of the milling and reduction of ores, during the period of their beneficial use, under such rules and regulations as may be prescribed by the Secretary of the Interior, and subject to the laws of the State or Territory in which said forests are respectively situated.
notes: RepealsSection repealed by , , , effective on and after , insofar as applicable to the issuance of rights-of-way over, upon, under, and through the public lands and lands in the National Forest System.Pub. L. 94–579, title VII, § 706(a)90 Stat. 2793Oct. 21, 1976Oct. 21, 1976

Codification“National forests” and “forests” substituted in text for “forest reserves” and “reserves”, respectively, on authority of , , which provided that forest reserves shall hereafter be known as national forests.act Mar. 4, 1907, ch. 290734 Stat. 1269

Savings ProvisionRepeal by , , , insofar as applicable to the issuance of rights-of-way, not to be construed as terminating any valid lease, permit, patent, etc., existing on , see note set out under , Public Lands.Pub.
